L1.area: XML-RPC response handling and deserialization layer

L2.description: An XML external entity (XXE) vulnerability exists where the XML parser used to process XML-RPC responses does not disable external entity resolution or doctype declarations. An attacker who can control the XML-RPC response content can exploit this to read local files, perform server-side request forgery, or cause denial of service.

L3.area: XML-RPC response parsing and document construction logic
L3.description: An XML external entity (XXE) vulnerability exists in the XML-RPC response parsing logic where the DocumentBuilderFactory used to parse incoming responses is not configured to disallow doctype declarations or disable external entity expansion. An attacker who can supply a crafted XML-RPC response can exploit this to read arbitrary files or perform server-side request forgery.

ground_truth.reason: The XML-RPC response parser creates a DocumentBuilderFactory without disabling external entity resolution or doctype declarations, allowing an attacker to inject XML external entities via crafted responses.
